what: Pull per-block walk times and population
title: The amenity basket
code: richmond <- close_places(close, "Richmond")$results[[1]] center <- list(lon = richmond$lon, lat = richmond$lat)  rows <- close_records(close_blocks_query, close, center = center, radius_m = 2500,                       mode = "walk", type = unname(basket),                       include_population = TRUE)  times <- list(); pop <- c() for (r in rows) {   times[[r$geoid]][[as.character(r$dest_type_id)]] <- r$travel_time   pop[r$geoid] <- if (is.null(r$population)) 0 else r$population }

title: The amenity basket
text: local free: Parks restaurants widespread; grocery frequent transit binding constraints.
code:
covered <- function(g, type_id) {   t <- times[[g]][[as.character(type_id)]]   !is.null(t) && t <= threshold } total <- sum(pop)  for (nm in names(basket)) {   share <- sum(pop[vapply(names(times), covered, logical(1), basket[[nm]])]) / total   cat(sprintf("%-18s %4.0f%%
", nm, 100 * share)) }  fully <- Filter(function(g) all(vapply(basket, function(ti) covered(g, ti),                                        logical(1))), names(times)) cat(sprintf("
All six: %.0f%% of residents
", 100 * sum(pop[unlist(fully)]) / total))

what: Which amenities to add, and where
title: The amenity basket
text: new facility type placed block covers everything within 15-minute walk — direction = "" walk isochrone (1 token ). greedily pick five sites turn currently-uncovered residents fully-covered ones: Loop greedy selection candidate walksheds, subtracting chosen site’s newly-covered residents picking next — top five ranked, mapped interventions.
code: library(sf) blocks <- close_as_sf(close_blocks_query(close, center = center, radius_m = 2500,                                          mode = "walk", type = 30), fetch = TRUE) cent <- st_coordinates(st_point_on_surface(blocks)) rownames(cent) <- blocks$geoid  missing1 <- function(g) {                # blocks missing exactly one amenity   miss <- Filter(function(ti) !covered(g, ti), basket)   if (length(miss) == 1) names(basket)[match(miss[[1]], basket)] else NA }  candidates <- Filter(function(g) !is.na(missing1(g)) && g %in% blocks$geoid,                      names(times)) candidates <- head(candidates[order(-pop[unlist(candidates)])], 25)  walkshed <- function(g) {   iso <- close_isochrone(close, lon = cent[g, 1], lat = cent[g, 2], mode = "walk",                          direction = "from", minutes = threshold, format = "blocks")   vapply(iso$data$blocks, function(b) b$geoid, character(1)) }

title: The amenity basket
text: close_places: free. close_blocks_query disc (~700 blocks x 6 categories): ~4,000 tokens. 25 candidate isochrones: ~25 tokens. Just inside 5,000-token month. Shrink radius_m candidate count trade coverage budget.

title: Get started with closecity
code: library(closecity)  # The catalog and health routes are free; data routes need a key from # https://account.close.city close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key_here")

title: Get started with closecity
text: Grocery 30, restaurants 27, cafes 31, libraries 43, parks 63, frequent-transit stops 61.
code: close <- close_client() close_modes(close)$data$modes close_last_updated(close)$data$last_updated  # Resolve a city name to its census place GEOID + centroid: close_places(close, "Providence")$results[[1]]  # The destination-type ids the data routes filter on: types <- close_destination_types(close)$data$destination_types

title: Get started with closecity
text: Metering 1 token per returned row (minimum 1 per request); isochrones exception 1 token per contour. 304 revalidation free.
code: summary <- close_block_summary(close, "250173523004004", mode = "walk") for (row in summary$results) {   cat(row$dest_type_id, row$mode, row$travel_time, "\n") }  # Metering is surfaced on every metered reply: cat(summary$tokens_charged, "charged;", summary$tokens_remaining, "left\n")

title: Get started with closecity
text: Paginated endpoints take cursor; loop close_records():
code: pois <- close_records(close_pois_search, close,                       lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1500, type = 31) length(pois)

title: Get started with closecity
code: first <- close_block_summary(close, "250173523004004") again <- close_block_summary(close, "250173523004004", if_none_match = first$etag) again$not_modified   # TRUE -- nothing was charged

title: Get started with closecity
text: Problem+JSON responses become classed conditions:
code: tryCatch(   close_block_summary(close, "000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) cat(e$status, e$slug, "\n") )

title: Get started with closecity
text: sf installed, convert reply sf object: POIs become points isochrones become polygons offline; block replies join census-block boundaries (pass block_geometry = fetch = TRUE).
code: pts <- close_as_sf(close_pois_search(close, lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412,                                      radius_m = 1500)) iso <- close_as_sf(close_isochrone(close, block = "440070036001010", minutes = 15))

what: Pick the business and its industry
title: Competitor walksheds
code: library(closecity) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key_here")  CAFE <- 31 providence <- close_places(close, "Providence")$results[[1]]  cafes <- close_records(close_pois_search, close,                        lat = providence$lat, lon = providence$lon,                        radius_m = 1200, type = CAFE) ours <- cafes[[1]] competitors <- cafes[2:6] ours$name

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Every block can reach shop within 10-minute walk:
code: walkshed <- function(dest_id) {   rows <- close_records(close_poi_catchment, close, dest_id,                         mode = "walk", max_minutes = 10)   unique(vapply(rows, function(r) r$geoid, character(1))) }  ours_shed <- walkshed(ours$dest_id) length(ours_shed)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Local set math, free catchments pulled:
code: for (c in competitors) {   shed <- walkshed(c$dest_id)   shared <- intersect(ours_shed, shed)   cat(sprintf("%-30s shares %3d blocks (%.0f%% of ours)\n",               c$name, length(shared), 100 * length(shared) / length(ours_shed))) }

title: Competitor walksheds
code: library(sf) pts <- close_as_sf(close_pois_search(close, lat = providence$lat,                                      lon = providence$lon, radius_m = 1200,                                      type = CAFE)) shed_blocks <- close_as_sf(close_poi_catchment(close, ours$dest_id, mode = "walk",                                                max_minutes = 10), fetch = TRUE)  plot(st_geometry(shed_blocks), col = "#eef0f7", border = "#c6cbe0") plot(st_geometry(pts), add = TRUE, col = "#202a5b", pch = 19) plot(st_geometry(pts[pts$dest_id == ours$dest_id, ]), add = TRUE,      col = "#f36e21", pch = 19, cex = 1.6)

what: Scaling to a county or state
title: Competitor walksheds
text: recipe runs wider area — search bounding box instead radius — catchment pulls metered per block, county-wide sweep can run thousands tokens. Keep max_minutes small page batches.

title: Competitor walksheds
text: close_places + one close_pois_search: free lookup + dozen tokens. Six 10-minute walk catchments (~50-150 blocks ): ~600 tokens. Well inside 5,000-token month single business near neighbours.

what: Find the place and the type ids
title: Looking for a home
text: lookups free (tokens):
code: library(closecity) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key_here")  geoid <- close_places(close, "Somerville")$results[[1]]$geoid GROCERY <- 30; RESTAURANT <- 27; FREQ_TRANSIT <- 61

title: Looking for a home
code: rows <- close_records(close_place_blocks, close, geoid, mode = "walk",                       type = c(GROCERY, RESTAURANT, FREQ_TRANSIT))  # Pivot to one record per block. by_block <- list() for (r in rows) {   g <- r$geoid   by_block[[g]][[as.character(r$dest_type_id)]] <- r$travel_time }  meets <- function(t) {   g <- t[["30"]]; r <- t[["27"]]; ft <- t[["61"]]   !is.null(g) && g <= 10 && !is.null(r) && r <= 5 && !is.null(ft) && ft <= 20 } candidates <- names(Filter(meets, by_block)) length(candidates)

title: Looking for a home
text: Block replies carry GEOIDs, join census-block boundaries — sf tigris installed, close_as_sf(..., fetch = TRUE) pulls :
code: library(sf) blocks <- close_as_sf(close_place_blocks(close, geoid, mode = "walk",                                          type = GROCERY), fetch = TRUE) hits <- blocks[blocks$geoid %in% candidates, ] plot(st_geometry(hits), col = "#f36e21", border = NA)

what: Narrow to the overlap of two commutes
title: Looking for a home
text: 20-minute transit isochrone workplace one token per contour: blocks chosen walkable groceries, food, frequent transit reasonable transit commute workers.
code: kendall  <- close_as_sf(close_isochrone(close, lon = -71.0865, lat = 42.3625,                                         mode = "transit", direction = "from",                                         minutes = 20)) downtown <- close_as_sf(close_isochrone(close, lon = -71.0589, lat = 42.3555,                                         mode = "transit", direction = "from",                                         minutes = 20))  commute_overlap <- st_intersection(st_union(kendall), st_union(downtown)) chosen <- hits[st_intersects(hits, commute_overlap, sparse = FALSE)[, 1], ] plot(st_geometry(chosen), col = "#058040", border = NA)

title: Looking for a home
text: close_places + close_destination_types: free. close_place_blocks Somerville (~800 blocks x 3 categories): ~2,400 tokens. Two transit isochrones: ~2 tokens. Comfortably inside 5,000-token month. larger city, swap close_place_blocks() bounded disc: close_blocks_query(close, center = list(lon = , lat = ), radius_m = 2500, ...).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Henry N (2026). closecity: Client Close API (api.close.city). R package version 1.0.0, https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r.
code: @Manual{,   title = {closecity: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)},   author = {Nathaniel Henry},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r}, }

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: R client Close API — travel times every US census block nearby points interest, walking, biking, public transit, data behind close.city, Close API. Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("henryspatialanalysis/closecity-r")

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Free routes (catalog, place lookup, health) need key:
code: library(closecity)  # The API key (ck_live_ / ck_test_) is created at https://account.close.city. client <- close_client("ck_live_your_key_here")  # Fastest travel time to each category from a census block. summary <- close_block_summary(client, "410390020001010", mode = c("walk", "transit")) for (row in summary$results) {   cat(row$dest_type_id, row$mode, row$travel_time, "\n") }  # Metering is surfaced on every metered reply. cat(summary$tokens_charged, "charged;", summary$tokens_remaining, "left\n") close_modes(close_client())$data$modes close_last_updated(close_client())$data$last_updated  # Resolve a city name to its census place GEOID + centroid: close_places(close_client(), "Providence")$results[[1]]

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: List endpoints keyset-paginated. close_records() follows opaque cursor returns every record: fetch single page read metadata directly: Cursors opaque signed — never construct modify .
code: pois <- close_records(close_pois_search, client,                       lat = 44.05, lon = -123.09, radius_m = 2000) vapply(pois, function(p) p$name, character(1)) page <- close_block_pois(client, "410390020001010", limit = 500) cat(length(page$results), "rows;", page$tokens_remaining, "tokens left\n") page$next_cursor   # pass back as `cursor =` for the next page

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Metered GETs return ETag. Send back revalidate free — 304 costs tokens, even zero balance:
code: first <- close_block_summary(client, "410390020001010") again <- close_block_summary(client, "410390020001010", if_none_match = first$etag) if (isTRUE(again$not_modified)) {   # your cached copy is still current; nothing was charged }

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: iso <- close_isochrone(client, block = "410390020001010",                        contours = c(15, 30, 45), mode = "walk", direction = "to") for (f in iso$data$features) {   cat(f$properties$contour, f$properties$reachable_blocks, "\n") }

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Errors RFC 9457 problem+json, raised classed conditions. Catch base close_api_error specific close_api_<slug>; every condition carries slug, status, title, detail, request_id, (present) retry_after:
code: tryCatch(   close_block_summary(client, "000000000000000"),   close_api_tokens_exhausted = function(e) message("buy more tokens"),   close_api_error = function(e) {     message(sprintf("%s (%d) — request %s", e$slug, e$status, e$request_id))   } )

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: sf installed, convert reply sf object — POIs become points, isochrones become polygons, block replies join census-block boundaries (tigris):
code: pts <- close_as_sf(close_pois_search(client, lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412,                                      radius_m = 1500)) iso <- close_as_sf(close_isochrone(client, block = "440070036001010", minutes = 15))

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/ Interactive API: https://api.close.city/docs Machine-readable contract: https://api.close.city/openapi.json

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: # with the deps installed (httr2, jsonlite, rlang, testthat): testthat::test_local(".")   # unit tests, no network (httr2 mocking)

what: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: Detects geometry payload: POI replies (close_pois_search, close_block_pois, close_point_pois, close_poi) become points lat/lon; isochrone close_isochrone(format = "geojson") reply becomes polygons; block replies (close_block_summary, close_blocks_query, close_place_blocks, close_poi_catchment) carry GEOIDs, join census-block boundaries via block_geometry (sf keyed geoid_col) let fetch tigris fetch = TRUE.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: close_as_sf(   x,   block_geometry = NULL,   geoid_col = "GEOID20",   crs = 4326,   fetch = FALSE )

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: x close_reply (list shape). block_geometry Optional sf block boundaries geoid_col column, joined block replies 15-digit GEOID. geoid_col Name GEOID column block_geometry. Default "GEOID20" (TIGER 2020 blocks). crs Coordinate reference system point/polygon geometry. Default 4326. fetch TRUE block_geometry NULL, pull needed TIGER blocks tigris (inferring state/county GEOIDs).

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: sf data frame.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close_as_sf(close_pois_search(client, lat = 41.82, lon = -71.41, radius_m = 1500)) close_as_sf(close_isochrone(client, block = "440070036001010", minutes = 15)) } # }

what: Nearby POIs and their travel time from a block — close_block_pois
title: Nearby POIs and their travel time from a block — close_block_pois
text: Every nearby POI travel time block, one row per (POI, mode). Paginated: loop close_records(). Metered per returned row.

title: Nearby POIs and their travel time from a block — close_block_pois
code: close_block_pois(   client,   geoid,   mode = NULL,   type = NULL,   dest_id = NULL,   max_minutes = NULL,   limit = NULL,   cursor = NULL )

title: Nearby POIs and their travel time from a block — close_block_pois
text: client close_client(). geoid 15-digit census block GEOID. mode Mode label(s) filter . type Destination type id(s) filter . dest_id Restrict specific destination id(s). max_minutes Cap travel time (<= 30). limit Page size (<= 1000). cursor Opaque keyset cursor previous page's next_cursor; normally use close_records() instead setting hand.

title: Nearby POIs and their travel time from a block — close_block_pois
text: close_reply.

what: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
title: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
text: Fastest travel time destination category census block, mode. Metered: one token per returned (category, mode) row.

title: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
code: close_block_summary(   client,   geoid,   mode = NULL,   type = NULL,   if_none_match = NULL )

title: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
text: client close_client(). geoid 15-digit census block GEOID. mode Mode label(s) filter ("walk", "bike", "transit"). type Destination type id(s) filter (see close_destination_types()). if_none_match ETag revalidate; returns free HTTP 304 match.

title: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
text: close_reply.

title: Fastest travel time from a block to each destination category — close_block_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cl <- close_client("ck_live_...") close_block_summary(cl, "250173523004004", mode = "walk") } # }

what: Per-block travel times within a polygon or radius — close_blocks_query
title: Per-block travel times within a polygon or radius — close_blocks_query
text: Blocks within GeoJSON polygon center + radius_m, one row per (block, category, mode). Paginated cursor carried request body. Metered per returned row.

title: Per-block travel times within a polygon or radius — close_blocks_query
code: close_blocks_query(   client,   polygon = NULL,   center = NULL,   radius_m = NULL,   type = NULL,   mode = NULL,   include_population = NULL,   limit = NULL,   cursor = NULL )

title: Per-block travel times within a polygon or radius — close_blocks_query
text: client close_client(). polygon GeoJSON Polygon/MultiPolygon (list). center list(lon =, lat =) centre, used radius_m. radius_m Radius metres (<= 28000). type Destination type id(s) filter . mode Mode label(s) filter . include_population Add block's population rows. limit Page size (<= 1000). cursor Opaque keyset cursor; normally use close_records() instead.

title: Per-block travel times within a polygon or radius — close_blocks_query
text: close_reply.

title: Travel-time contours (isochrone) — close_isochrone
text: Travel-time contours block (GEOID) lon + lat. Charged one token per contour level (1-4), per row. format = "geojson" reply carries polygon geometry can convert close_as_sf().

title: Travel-time contours (isochrone) — close_isochrone
code: close_isochrone(   client,   block = NULL,   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   mode = NULL,   direction = NULL,   minutes = NULL,   contours = NULL,   format = NULL,   v = NULL,   if_none_match = NULL )

title: Travel-time contours (isochrone) — close_isochrone
text: client close_client(). block Origin block GEOID (give lon + lat). lon, lat Origin point (WGS84), alternative block. mode "walk", "bike", "transit". direction "" (blocks can reach origin) "". minutes single threshold (1-60). contours 4 ascending levels (vector comma string), instead minutes. format "geojson" (polygons) "blocks" (block list). v Opaque cache-buster, echoed back. if_none_match ETag revalidate; returns free HTTP 304 match.

title: Travel-time contours (isochrone) — close_isochrone
text: close_reply.

title: Search POIs by area — close_pois_search
text: Search points interest bounding box (bbox) radius (lat + lon + radius_m). Spatial (travel times). Paginated; loop close_records(). Metered per returned row.

title: Collect every record from a paginated endpoint — close_records

what: The reply object returned by every endpoint — close_reply
title: The reply object returned by every endpoint — close_reply
text: Every endpoint returns close_reply: S3-classed list carrying parsed body plus metering caching metadata first-class fields.

title: The reply object returned by every endpoint — close_reply
text: data parsed JSON body (list), NULL 304. results results array list endpoints (else empty list). next_cursor Keyset cursor next page, NULL. tokens_charged, tokens_remaining Token accounting; NULL free member-unmetered replies. etag Response ETag, pass back if_none_match free 304. status HTTP status code. not_modified TRUE free 304 revalidation (data NULL). request_id Server request id, useful support.

title: closecity 1.0.0
text: First public release closecity R client Close API (api.close.city). close_client() metered, read-public endpoints: catalog, block/point summaries POIs, POI search/detail/catchment, areal block queries, isochrones. First-class metering (tokens_charged / tokens_remaining), ETag/304 conditional requests, keyset pagination via close_records(), typed RFC 9457 errors classed conditions. close_places() place-name lookup (city/town GEOID + centroid). Opt-spatial output: close_as_sf() sf::st_as_sf() method (POI points, isochrone polygons, census-block joins).
